mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive returns occur in less than 50 percent of rate-cut periods.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer spending, which reduces corporate earnings expectations and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in greater than 65 percent of instances where unemployment rises above the threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous acceleration in retail sales and consumer price inflation triggers expectations of aggressive monetary tightening, which compresses equity valuations and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint high readings in retail sales and CPI yield positive forward returns in greater than 60 percent of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Expanding industrial production reflects strengthening manufacturing output and supply chain normalization, which supports near-term corporate revenue growth and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive industrial production growth coincides with negative 5-day forward returns in greater than 70 percent of observations.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts falling below 600 thousand units indicates a sharp contraction in residential construction demand, which reduces capital expenditure forecasts for related sectors and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in greater than 55 percent of months where housing starts drop below the threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirms robust labor market expansion, which sustains consumer spending and corporate profit growth, yielding positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative forward returns in greater than 60 percent of triggered months.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained federal funds rates above 4 percent combined with elevated consumer price inflation creates a stagflationary environment that erodes real corporate margins and triggers negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in greater than 50 percent of periods where both rate and inflation thresholds are breached.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real gross domestic product reflects broad-based economic expansion that directly translates into higher corporate earnings forecasts, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive GDP growth coincides with negative 5-day forward returns in greater than 65 percent of quarterly observations.
